sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the green dot is still at [30,20]-[31,21] (or nearby). It didn't move RIGHT through the divider. The green dot is still inside the dark red box at [30,18]-[33,21].
Wait, the image shows the green dot at the same position as before. Let me check the segmentation to see where the green dot is now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check green dot position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        r1, c1 = min(p[0] for p in b), min(p[1] for p in b)
        r2, c2 = max(p[0] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Green dot: id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{r1},{c1}]-[{r2},{c2}]")

# Check dark red box
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'r':
        b = n['boundary']
        r1, c1 = min(p[0] for p in b), min(p[1] for p in b)
        r2, c2 = max(p[0] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"Dark red: id={n['id']}, px={n['pixels']}, bbox=[{r1},{c1}]-[{r2},{c2}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:

Dark red: id=24, px=12, bbox=[30,18]-[33,21]

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green dot is still at [30,20]-[31,21], inside the dark red box at [30,18]-[33,21]. The green dot didn't move RIGHT despite 6 RIGHT actions. This means the green dot is blocked by the dark red box boundary.
The dark red box spans cols 18-21, and the green dot is at cols 20-21. The right edge of the green dot is at col 21, which is the right edge of the dark red box. So the green dot can't move further RIGHT because it's blocked by the box.
I need to find another way to get the green dot out of the dark red box. Maybe I need to click on something to open the box, or move the green dot to a different position within the box.
Wait, looking at the image, the green dot is at the top-right corner of the dark red box. The dark red box spans rows 30-33, cols 18-21. The green dot is at rows 30-31, cols 20-21.
Maybe I need to move the green dot DOWN to exit the box from the bottom, or LEFT to exit from the left.
But the goal is to reach the right side, so moving LEFT doesn't help. Let me try moving the green dot DOWN to see if it can exit the box from the bottom.
Actually, wait. Looking at the image more carefully, the green dot is at the top-right of the dark red box. The dark red box is at [30,18]-[33,21], which is rows 30-33, cols 18-21. The green dot is at [30,20]-[31,21], which is inside the box.
If I move the green dot DOWN, it would move to [32,20]-[33,21], which is still inside the box. If I move it further DOWN, it might exit the box at the bottom.
But the box ends at row 33, so moving DOWN from [32,20]-[33,21] would put the green dot at [34,20]-[35,21], which is outside the box.
Let me try moving the green dot DOWN to exit the box.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- Green dot at [30,20]-[31,21], blocked at right edge of dark red box [30,18]-[33,21]
- 6 RIGHT actions had no effect - green dot can't exit box from right side
- Need to find another way to exit the box
Plan: Try moving DOWN to exit box from bottom, then navigate to divider

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move green dot DOWN to exit box from bottom
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
